what_changed: Started with no defined operations, ended with one minimal operation identified as the simplest possible starting point after ruling out alternatives.
Went from treating two concepts as independent to showing each one produces the other in a loop.
Went from one network setup to confirming that a measured property stayed consistent across multiple different configurations.
Density was tested as a cause of a correlation and shown not to be — the hypothesis was ruled out.
Structural importance was tested as a cause of a correlation and shown not to be — the hypothesis was ruled out.
Went from one measurement method to multiple methods, finding that the rank order of results held up but the absolute values shifted depending on which estimator was used.
Went from seven separate metrics with unknown relationships to showing that most were redundant and one was essentially a mirror of another.
A phenomenon that looked real turned out to be a side effect of how the data was binned — it disappeared when the binning changed.
Went from listing specific constraints as the key description to showing that the overall shape of the constraint pattern mattered more than which constraints were present.
Went from treating distinctions as the basic unit to showing that what matters more is whether distinctions get carried forward.
Went from assuming patterns were objective features of the world to showing they depend on both the world and how you choose to represent it.
A causal approach to explanation was shown to fail most of the time, while other approaches worked better in many cases.
Went from assuming that building order mattered to confirming that all tested orderings produced the same result.
Four junction points went from having an unknown relationship to being shown as a linked chain where each depends on the previous one.
An apparent progression across audits was revealed to be an artifact of the order in which audits were conducted rather than genuine change.
What looked like steady forward movement turned out to be a repeating cycle through the same states.
Went from not knowing whether back-and-forth exchange between elements was necessary to finding that every observed improvement involved it.
Four separate elements were tested in combination, and a specific all-four-present configuration was identified as the only arrangement that sustained novelty over time.
Objects went from being modeled as static things with fixed properties to being modeled as the accumulated residue of past exchanges.
A single claim about layered persistence was re-evaluated and found to have no additional support — the re-check did not strengthen or weaken the original claim.
